page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is a DSv3-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 35 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 393 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance split between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls in the bottom 25% tier, as do single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. However, the 48-vCPU configuration elevates multi-core performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores to the middle 50% average tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block reads/writes, consistently register in the average tier.

The D48s_v3 is suited for workloads that benefit from high core counts and parallel processing rather than raw single-thread speed. Factual benchmark results show average performance for database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. Large-scale LLM inference is weak, though smaller model inference achieves average throughput. Qualitatively, the server offers balanced resource density for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded compilation, and database hosting, though the older processor architecture presents a tradeoff in single-core efficiency.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 393 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark data indicates a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the bottom 25% tier, whereas multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently reach the average middle 50% tier. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database hosting that leverage parallel processing rather than high single-thread velocity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is a DSv3-family virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 393 GB local storage. It delivers average multi-core and database performance alongside weaker single-core speeds.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is a DSv3 virtual machine with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 393 GB local storage, offering balanced multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs, premium storage capabilities, and stable multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB RAM
Includes 393 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Shows lower single-core and single-threaded execution speeds
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded tasks
